label: Uneasy calm
summary: The world held at an uneasy 41 as the pause in direct US-Iran fighting stretched into the weekend but the rhetoric around it turned sharply hostile. With open strikes still quiet and Qatari and Pakistani mediators working to revive talks, President Trump warned that '1,000 missiles are locked and loaded' and that Washington would 'decimate and destroy' Iran over an alleged assassination plot, keeping the region on edge even without new attacks. Violence surfaced elsewhere: a shooting at Toronto's crowded Salsa on St. Clair festival killed two and wounded six among 13,000 revellers, a Russian guided-bomb strike on Sumy killed five including a child, and Sudan's RSF pressed its drone siege of El Obeid, where UN investigators fear an el-Fasher-style massacre for half a million trapped civilians. Nature ran heavy too. Typhoon Bavi made landfall in Zhejiang after nearly two million people were evacuated across eastern China, having already triggered landslides that killed at least 15 in the southern Philippines, while a 1-in-1,000-year deluge in southeast Missouri killed one and forced 361 rescues, including 250 airlifted from a flooded summer camp. The humanitarian ledger stayed grim as DR Congo's fast-moving Ebola outbreak passed 625 deaths and Venezuela's June earthquake toll neared 3,900. Against that, Friday's Wall Street close still stood as the week's steadier signal, with the S&P 500 up 0.42 percent to 7,575 on SK Hynix's record debut, and a run of good news held the floor: a 99-percent-accurate blood test for womb cancer, the accelerating rollout of twice-yearly HIV-prevention shots across Africa, and renewables meeting all of 2025's global electricity growth. The headline lands at 41, matching yesterday: a fighting pause and firm markets on one side, escalatory threats, a deadly typhoon and a worsening outbreak on the other.

drivers.headline: Direct US-Iran fighting stays paused into the weekend after two days of exchanged strikes, as Qatari and Pakistani mediators keep working to de-escalate and revive negotiations, and Washington signals it will continue talking to Tehran even as it insists the earlier ceasefire is 'over,' preserving the first sustained lull since the truce frayed mid-week

President Trump sharply escalates the rhetoric around the Iran standoff, warning that '1,000 missiles are locked and loaded' and that the United States would 'decimate and destroy' Iran if Tehran attempts to assassinate him, saying orders have already been given, an inflammatory threat that rattles a region still holding its breath after this month's exchanged strikes

A shooting erupts at Toronto's Salsa on St. Clair festival, where roughly 13,000 people had gathered to celebrate Latin culture, leaving two men dead and six others with gunshot wounds; police recover two firearms across three crime scenes, make no arrests, and describe an exchange of gunfire between individuals as Mayor Olivia Chow condemns 'a reckless, despicable act of violence at a crowded festival'

The Russia-Ukraine war grinds on with 215 combat clashes recorded on July 11, as a Russian guided-bomb strike on Sumy kills five people including a 13-year-old girl and injures 32 near a public transport stop, and half of a dozen missiles fired overnight evade Ukrainian air defences, even as Kyiv's drones strike Russian fuel supply lines and shadow-fleet tankers

Sudan's Rapid Support Forces intensify 'relentless' drone attacks on El Obeid, the North Kordofan capital where roughly half a million civilians are trapped, damaging at least 16 civilian and service sites including hospitals, schools and power stations, as UN human rights chief Volker Turk and investigators warn the city risks becoming the next el-Fasher-style massacre and press for urgent international action

Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall late on July 11 in Yuhuan, Zhejiang, with maximum sustained winds near 144kph after nearly two million people were evacuated across eastern China and neighbouring Fujian deployed more than 17,000 rescue personnel, prompting China's first Red Rainstorm Warning of 2026 as authorities brace for flooding, landslides and storm surge along the densely populated coast

Landslides and flooding whipped up by Typhoon Bavi's enhanced monsoon rains kill at least 15 people, with nine still missing, across the southern Philippines, including ten buried in a landslide in Sarangani's Malapatan town and five killed in Lanao del Sur, as more than half a million people are affected and over 11,000 are forced from their homes even though the storm never made landfall there

A 1-in-1,000-year deluge dumps nearly a foot of rain in under 24 hours on southeast Missouri, killing 23-year-old Faith Gregory, swept from near her Davisville home, and forcing 361 rescues across Iron, Reynolds and Crawford counties, among them about 250 children and staff airlifted by National Guard Black Hawks from a flooded Camp Taum Sauk as the Black River surged to a record 28.7 feet

US stocks close higher on Friday to cap a choppy week, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.42 percent to 7,575.39, the Dow Jones Industrial Average adding 0.29 percent to 52,637.01 and the Nasdaq Composite rising 0.29 percent to 26,281.61, as chipmaker SK Hynix pops in a record Wall Street debut that tested the AI-memory trade while investors tracked the US-Iran de-escalation

The Democratic Republic of the Congo's Ebola outbreak, the fastest-growing the continent has seen, passes 625 deaths against about 1,792 confirmed cases, with Ituri province still the epicentre and suspected cases now recorded in previously untouched Tshopo and Haut-Uele provinces, as the WHO warns the Bundibugyo-virus epidemic is not yet under control and a clinical trial of two candidate treatments gets under way

Venezuela remains in mourning as the official death toll from the June 24 twin earthquakes, magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 and only 39 seconds apart, climbs toward 3,899 with more than 16,700 injured and nearly 18,000 left homeless, the country's deadliest natural disaster in over a century, as officials concede the figure is likely a vast undercount and the interim government requests 4.85 billion dollars from the IMF for reconstruction

A run of good news holds the floor against the week's heavier stories, from Britain's PinPoint blood test detecting gynaecological cancers with 99 percent accuracy and potentially sparing thousands of women invasive procedures, to twice-yearly lenacapavir HIV-prevention shots accelerating across Africa, to confirmation that renewables met the entirety of global electricity demand growth in 2025 with solar up 30 percent

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 Iran war / 2026 Iran war ceasefire / Twelve-Day War ceasefire / Typhoon Bavi 2026 / 2026 Venezuela earthquakes / 2026 Ebola epidemic / Gaza peace plan)
